reported_items.headline: AssemblyAI Voice Agent API on Universal-3 Pro Streaming, 307ms P50, $4.50/hr
Deepgram Flux posts lowest end-of-speech detection latency
ElevenLabs cross-lingual emotion/performance transfer in dubbing
Garry Tan: retrieval latency is the voice-agent bottleneck; Moss does sub-10ms in-process search (open source)
Gradium raises $70M seed for expressive voice models
gpt-realtime: trained with customers; MultiChallenge 30.5% (vs 20.6%), ComplexFuncBench 66.5% (vs 49.7%)
Sesame launches consumer iOS app
Vapi hits ~$500M valuation, wins Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
Cartesia Sonic 2 deprecated June 1; Sonic 3.5 live across 42 languages, 100ms p90 TTFA, native alphanumeric handling
Developer signal: real voice moat is recovery loop (rollback, escalation, context) not the underlying model
Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 at $3/hr; live on Starlink support with 70% resolution rate and 20% sales conversion on 28 tools
LiveKit Inference runs full xAI voice pipeline (STT + Grok + TTS) under single API key
TML-Interaction-Small: full-duplex 200ms micro-turn interaction model, 77.8 FD-bench vs GPT-Realtime-2 46.8; interactivity as first-class architecture
AA-WER v2.0 AA-AgentTalk: agent-specific STT benchmark, 469 samples, 17 accent groups; ElevenLabs Scribe v2 leads at 2.3%
Gradium #1 on Coval TTS benchmark: 155ms P50 TTFA, 2ms IQR, 3.3% WER — beats Cartesia Sonic-3 and Deepgram Aura-2
Microsoft Build 2026: MAI-Voice 2 (15+ languages, wider emotion) and MAI-Transcribe 1.5 announced
Native S2S (Gemini Live $0.00165/min) shown up to ~12x cheaper than bundled pipeline APIs; S2S agentic benchmarks competitive
User: Sesame iOS turn-taking 'extremely impressive — very few false interruptions even when you pause'
ElevenLabs v3 GA: explicitly not for real-time (Flash v2.5 ~75ms recommended); quality-vs-latency fork documented in API
FDB-v3 benchmarks full-duplex agents on tool use under real disfluency; GPT-Realtime leads accuracy (0.600), Gemini Live 3.1 fastest (4.25s); self-correction top failure mode across all systems
Microsoft launches MAI-Voice-2 (TTS, 15+ languages, emotional range incl. whisper) and MAI-Transcribe-1.5 at Build 2026 on Azure AI Foundry
Project Solara: agent-first hardware OS for voice-primary devices (smart badge, display), Azure cloud inference, Target/CVS/Best Buy pilots
Developer field signal: Bland AI real-world P50 ~800ms vs. claimed sub-400ms; 800ms threshold consistently cited as user churn point
MAI-Transcribe-1.5 at Build 2026: improved accuracy and noise robustness; pairs with MAI-Voice-2 as integrated STT+TTS stack in Azure Foundry
MAI-Voice-2 at Build 2026: 15 languages, emotion registers (angry/joyful/whispered), zero-shot cloning, edge inference, $22/1M chars; 72% preferred over predecessor
Project Solara: agent-first OS for wearable+desk devices where voice is primary interface; pilots at Best Buy, CVS Health, Target
Miso One: open-weights 8B TTS with 110ms latency, one-shot voice cloning, emotional range; free on GitHub
Voice agent testing tooling now a named category: 7+ platforms with audio-specific scoring (mispronunciation, pipeline latency, barge-in timing)

themes.theme: Value shifting up-stack to orchestration/reliability (Vapi, LiveKit); raw TTS/STT commoditizing.
Real-time competition is about latency + turn-taking + interruption, not voice quality (Deepgram Flux, AssemblyAI streaming, Cartesia Sonic).
Retrieval latency emerging as the next named bottleneck for voice agents (Garry Tan / Moss).
Speech-to-speech models (gpt-realtime) collapsing the STT->LLM->TTS chain; pressure on pure-play vendors.
Function/tool-calling accuracy in voice becoming a key benchmark axis (ComplexFuncBench).
Consumer voice apps (Sesame) testing voice-as-destination vs voice-as-feature.
Real-time competition is about latency + turn-taking + interruption, not voice quality.
Retrieval latency emerging as a named bottleneck for voice agents (Garry Tan / Moss).
Speech-to-speech models collapsing the STT->LLM->TTS chain; pressure on pure-play vendors.
Function/tool-calling accuracy in voice becoming a key benchmark axis.
Interactivity-as-architecture (not software harness) emerging as differentiation axis; full-duplex quality gap vs raw latency gap (TML).
xAI price pressure ($3/hr Grok Voice) forcing value-justification at OpenAI Realtime-2 price points.
Full-stack bundling (STT+LLM+TTS under one key via LiveKit/Deepgram/AssemblyAI) becoming the developer default; orchestration layers as assembly points.
Recovery loop (error handling, rollback, escalation) named by builders as the real defensibility gap in voice agents.
Latency consistency (IQR / tail latency) emerging as the key production metric above raw P50 speed.
Speech-to-speech models collapsing the STT->LLM->TTS chain; S2S cost barrier falling fast (Gemini Live $0.00165/min).
Consumer voice apps (Sesame) testing voice-as-destination; turn-taking quality is what users praise.
Microsoft building in-house voice stack (MAI series) — platform risk for pure-play TTS/STT vendors serving Azure enterprise.
Retrieval latency and session context persistence emerging as named bottlenecks for voice agents (Garry Tan / Moss; Solara dev community).
Function/tool-calling accuracy in voice becoming a key benchmark axis (ComplexFuncBench, FDB-v3); self-correction under disfluency now the top named failure mode.
Big Tech (Microsoft MAI-Voice-2, MAI-Transcribe-1.5) entering voice infra as bundled Azure services; compressing margin window for pure-play STT/TTS vendors.
Voice as primary hardware I/O (Project Solara smart badge/display) signaling enterprise device market; watch retail + healthcare verticals.
TTS market bifurcating: expressive/high-fidelity models (ElevenLabs v3) vs. real-time models (Flash v2.5, Cartesia Sonic) as distinct product categories.
Real-time competition is about latency + turn-taking + interruption, not voice quality (Deepgram Flux, AssemblyAI streaming, Cartesia Sonic 4 at 40ms TTFA).
Retrieval latency emerging as the next named bottleneck for voice agents (Garry Tan / Moss; YC hackathon June 6-7).
Open-source TTS (Voxtral, MOSS-TTS-Nano, Miso One) running sustained release cadence; demolishing commercial TTS pricing floor in the mid-tier.
Microsoft entering full-stack voice AI (MAI-Voice-2 + MAI-Transcribe-1.5 + Solara) changes enterprise procurement dynamics for specialist TTS/STT vendors.
Voice agent evaluation/testing tooling (Bluejay, Coval, Cekura, Maxim) becoming production-critical infrastructure as production latency gaps emerge.
Agent-first device platforms (Project Solara) positioning voice as the default enterprise computing interface — TAM expansion signal.

notes: Dedupe key = normalized headline/url. reported_items entry: {id, date, company, headline, url, type, status}. type = tech | customer_signal | founder_take | market | funding. status = new | developing | closed. themes = rolling directional observations; direction = up | down | emerging | watch.
